notes: Assertion 'codebase-grounded' passes 100% in both configs — baseline Claude explores the codebase well regardless of skill. Not a differentiator.
All other assertions (7/8) differentiate perfectly: 100% with_skill vs 0% without_skill.

The baseline produces solid technical designs that follow codebase conventions, but lacks: structured decision tracking, formal edge case analysis, PRD traceability, adversarial review with verdict taxonomy, and pipeline integration.

With-skill runs take ~93% longer (510s vs 265s) and use ~70% more tokens (97k vs 57k). The increase comes from deeper interviews (16-31 questions vs 8-10), full 10-domain coverage, and adversarial review.
Graders consistently note the assertions test 'skill-shaped outputs' rather than 'output quality' — the baseline designs are substantively good but structurally non-conforming.
Waivers with-skill achieved 100% PRD coverage (all 31 FRs + 7 stories traced to behavior specs). This traceability is unique to the skill.
